summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms deserialization RCE via object injection. A crafted serialized object payload uploaded through Etemplate request processing successfully executes arbitrary commands via __wakeup() gadget chain.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate RCE by uploading a malicious serialized object and triggering its deserialization, resulting in command execution inside the container.
actual_observed_result: Executing ./reproduction/run.sh built the EGroupware container, uploaded a crafted serialized object payload, triggered deserialization via Etemplate request, and achieved command execution creating proof artifacts.
verification_method: The PoC script checks for: (1) successful file upload, (2) deserialization triggered, (3) command execution evidence, (4) proof file created by executed command. Verification distinguishes RCE from upload/parse errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: PHP object instantiated with injected payload
Proof file created: /tmp/deserialization_rce_proof.txt with command output

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the EGroupware repository, checks out the vulnerable commit, and mounts the checkout into the container at /target-repo.
